title: Strobilanthes viridis
intro: Strobilanthes viridis, commonly known as the green flowered Strobilanthes, is a medicinal plant belonging to the family Acanthaceae. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it useful in treating various ailments such as fever, cough, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Strobilanthes viridis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from Strobilanthes viridis include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to alleviate symptoms of fever, respiratory issues, and digestive problems.
taxonomy: Strobilanthes viridis is classified under the family Acanthaceae. This family comprises flowering plants commonly known as acanthus or thorny flower. The classification of Strobilanthes viridis into the Acanthaceae family is due to its similar characteristics and physical attributes to other plants within this family. The Acanthaceae family is further divided into 32 subfamilies, with Strobilanthes viridis belonging to the subfamily Lindiaceae. Strobilanthes viridis is also classified under the order Lamiales in the kingdom Plantae.
